channel.title: NASA 新闻 RSS（简体中文版）
channel.description: NASA 官方新闻 + 英译简

item.title: 百万面板项目
item.description:
犹他州的新太阳能和电池存储设施是该地区最大的设施之一。
历史上，犹他州中部的城堡谷一直是煤炭中心，自 1800 年代末以来，瓦萨奇高原向西的山坡上的采矿作业一直很活跃。 2026 年 6 月，该地区迎来了一项不同的能源开发项目，当时盐湖城东南约 130 英里（210 公里）处的阳光谷一座大型太阳能发电和电池存储工厂投入使用。
最近建成的绿河能源中心，如上图（右）的 Landsat 8 所示，在几平方英里的先前未开发土地上配备了近一百万个太阳能电池板和大约 500 个电池。该设施拥有 400 兆瓦的太阳能发电能力和另外 400 兆瓦的电池存储能力。这使其成为美国众多公用事业规模太阳能和电池存储项目之一。能源信息管理局预计将于 2026 年并入该国电网。
据新闻报道，犹他州的该设施预计将为盐湖城和该州其他地区供电，项目工作人员估计它可以为超过 10 万户家庭提供足够的电力。凭借其集成的电池存储，该发电厂有可能全天候发电，即使在没有阳光的情况下也是如此。绿河能源中心可以利用最初为该地区燃煤发电厂建造的现有输电线路，以城堡谷的能源遗产为基础。
尽管犹他州采用煤炭作为其州石，并长期依赖煤炭作为能源，但太阳能和地热等其他能源正在成为该州能源结构的重要组成部分。到 2025 年，煤炭发电占该州发电量的一半左右，低于 2015 年的约 75%。同时，太阳能发电量在 2025 年占发电量的约 14%，而十年前几乎为零。卫星数据可帮助参与能源转型的规划者和政策制定者评估可再生能源系统的潜力并跟踪其采用和绩效。
NASA 地球观测站图像由 Lauren Dauphin 使用来自美国的陆地卫星数据拍摄地质调查。 林赛·杜尔曼的故事。
当我们探索宇宙并发现更多关于我们的家园星球时，请随时了解 NASA 的最新内容。
最近的一项分析揭示了夜间人造光在哪里增强以及在哪里减弱。
从地热热点到曾经的“太平洋灯塔”，热量在……的火山景观下蔓延。
一位宇航员在前往月球途中拍摄的照片，在小说中揭示了我们的星球及其在太空中的位置……
订阅地球观测站并将地球放入您的收件箱中。
美国宇航局地球观测站每天为您带来地球的深入故事和令人惊叹的图像。
开放访问 NASA 的地球科学数据档案

item.title: 好奇心博客，Sols 4954–4960：庆祝我们过去和现在的漫游车工程师
item.description:
火星科学实验室任务团队成员
作者：Lucy Thompson，加拿大新不伦瑞克大学高级研究科学家
地球规划日期：2026 年 7 月 27 日星期五
作为 APXS 上行链路领导和战略规划人员，我有幸在运营期间的大部分时间与流动站工程师一起工作。 APXS 仪器测量岩石、松散材料和大气的化学成分，位于好奇号机械臂的末端。这意味着我们希望分析的任何感兴趣的目标都必须能够安全地部署手臂、APXS 和 MAHLI（特写成像仪）。因此，我们依靠流动站工程师进行评估并确定手臂移动的顺序，以将我们安全地放置在目标上。最近，我们的工作空间布满灰尘，但漫游车工程师已经成功找到了他们能够清理的区域并部署了 APXS 和 MAHLI。本周也不例外，尽管我们的一些工作空间在初步观察中显得不太理想。该团队设法找到了感兴趣的岩石目标 (x5)，流动站工程师能够安全地将手臂放在上面并刷洗，以便我们可以使用 APXS 和 MAHLI 对其进行分析。这确保了我们在继续攀登夏普山时，通过不同色调和纹理的岩层，跟踪潜在的化学变化以及沉积和蚀变环境，获得高质量的成分数据和图像。
火星车工程师还负责安全地将好奇号驾驶到科学团队确定的感兴趣的区域。他们必须评估地形是否存在潜在危险，例如可能损坏流动站车轮的大型阻力块、我们可能被卡住的沙/土块以及流动站可能滑倒的高斜坡。尽管在任务初期车轮意外受损，并且在我们开始攀登夏普山时陷入了一些土壤/沙子中，但工程师们还是成功地引导我们安全行驶了超过 23 英里（37 公里）的行驶距离和超过 4,400 英尺（约 1.35 公里）的海拔高度。我们最近要求开车前往特定地点，以便拍摄团队认为可能是含硫酸镁/碳酸盐单元内的侵蚀表面的图像（请参阅本文随附的图像）。当然，工程师们能够满足我们的愿望，周一的计划中划掉了第一站，而本周末计划的行驶将带我们前往下一站。
流动站工程师还确保我们的钻探活动安全、成功地执行，并负责对将钻探样本传送到我们内部 CheMin 和 SAM 仪器所需的手臂运动进行排序。这需要在 2016 年电机发生故障后彻底重新配置我们的钻井方式，在恢复工作之前，我们在喷气推进实验室进行了近一年半的大量幕后工作。此后，好奇号已经钻探了 20 多个岩石目标。
因此，感谢火星车工程师以及好奇号团队的所有工程师和科学家，我们在盖尔陨石坑又进行了整整一周的活动。当我们攀登夏普山并利用 APXS、ChemCam、MAHLI 和 Mastcam 越来越接近雅丹单元时，我们将继续跟踪硫酸盐/碳酸盐单元的化学成分、质地、色调和沉积结构。好奇号还继续监测盖尔的当地环境和总体大气。
火星是距太阳第四远的行星，也是第七大行星。这是我们所知道的唯一有人居住的星球……
探索火星图像、视频、资源、PDF 和工具包的集合。发现旨在提供信息、教育和启发的有价值的内容……
每个被派往红色星球的机器人探险家都有其独特的由科学驱动的能力。一个…的许多属性
了解火星过去、现在或未来生命潜力的关键可以在美国宇航局的四个……

item.title: 寻找 NASA+ 现已在 Amazon Fire TV 上直播
item.description:
NASA+ 继续努力让太空离家更近，并正在转向更多的流媒体平台。周四，美国宇航局宣布其节目将在 Fire TV 频道播出。 Fire TV 客户可以通过在兼容设备上询问 Alexa+ 轻松访问此内容。
Fire TV 未来的节目可能包括科学任务发射、与人类着陆系统交会对接的试飞、机器人月球运送、阿尔忒弥斯下首次载人登月任务等等。与往常一样，NASA+ 仍然可以通过 NASA 应用程序和该机构的网站免费使用，没有广告。
“1958 年的《国家航空航天法案》要求我们与尽可能广泛的受众分享我们的太空探索故事，我们的流媒体合作伙伴帮助我们实现了这一目标，”华盛顿总部 NASA+ 总经理丽贝卡·西尔蒙斯 (Rebecca Sirmons) 说道。 “《阿尔忒弥斯 II》俘获了全球观众的心，激励了阿尔忒弥斯一代。在我们努力建设人类第一个月球基地的过程中，我们将继续在每一步提供透明、引人入胜的内容报道。”
今年早些时候，在其点火活动期间，美国宇航局分享了该机构大胆前进的计划，包括增加阿耳特弥斯到月球表面发射的频率，以及建造第一个月球基地，以及该机构的其他优先事项。此次发行合作伙伴关系是履行 NASA 承诺的又一步，即让公众更好地了解该机构，并在多种设备上为观众提供他们喜欢的频道上的任务和教育内容。
谢丽尔·沃纳华盛顿总部202-358-1600cheryl.m.warner@nasa.gov

item.title: 工作人员移动 Artemis IV 液氢罐
item.description: 2026 年 5 月 15 日，NASA 位于新奥尔良的米丘德装配厂的工作人员将 130 英尺高的 Artemis IV 液氢罐从主厂房内的生产单元运输到占地 829 英亩场地的独立测试大楼中。液氢罐将构成 SLS（太空发射系统）火箭核心级的一部分，提供数千加仑的超冷推进剂到四个 RS-25 引擎之一。

item.title: NASA 的 MAVEN 阐明了对火星极光的新认识
item.description: NASA MAVEN (Mars Atmosphere and Volatile Evolution) mission scientists have uncovered a key puzzle piece in understanding certain types of auroras on Mars, finding that they form in a similar way to Earth-based auroras.Results published Thursday inNature Communicationsshow the same mechanism that circulates and catapults charged particles into Earth’s atmosphere is happening at Mars on much smaller scales because of differences in the two planets’ magnetic fields.The MAVEN spacecraft, in orbit around Mars, experienced a loss of signal with ground stations on Earth on Dec. 6, 2025. On June 3, NASAdeclaredthe mission had concluded after finding the spacecraft to be unrecoverable. However, data from the mission is still being used to inform NASA science and future missions to Mars.When the Sun’s magnetic field lines get close to Earth’s magnetosphere, the large magnetic bubble protecting the planet, they can reconnect and inject energy and mass throughout Earth's magnetosphere andmagnetotail, ultimately firing electrons back into the atmosphere to generate Earth’s auroras. This process, called the Dungey cycle, drives electrical currents, accelerates charged particles that create auroras, and controls the circulation of plasma in Earth’s magnetosphere and ionosphere.This new study shows that a miniature version of the Dungey cycle is happening over Mars’ strong crustal magnetic fields, which gives scientists a better look into the physics of Martian auroras.“We knew that magnetic reconnection was happening at Mars but did not expect it to be like the Dungey cycle,” said Shaosui Xu, lead author of the study and associate research physicist at the Space Sciences Laboratory at the University of California, Berkeley.Mars does not have a global magnetic field like Earth. Earth’s magnetic field is created by our planet’s churning core, while Mars has numerous miniature magnetospheres that arise from intensely magnetized crust scattered around the planet. These regions were formed around 4 billion years ago when lava cooled in the presence of Mars’ ancient global magnetic field, which has since disappeared due tointense solar wind stripping the planet’s atmosphere.The MAVEN mission has observed highly localized auroras over these crustal fields, similar to Earth’s auroras at the poles, but it wasn’t until now that scientists could fully understand the physics of how they form. The study used several instruments aboard the MAVEN spacecraft to build up a picture of the Dungey-like behavior: the Magnetometer and Solar Wind Electron Analyzer instruments, which were used to determine the magnetic configuration and derive electrical currents, and the STATIC (Suprathermal and Thermal Ion Composition) instrument, which was used to measure plasma flows in the ionosphere.“We really pushed the limit of STATIC to get the data we needed,” said Xu. “It was the final piece to the puzzle in understanding these localized auroras.”The realization that a Dungey-like cycle was happening within these crustal magnetic fields answered the question of how the electrons were being energized to create the auroras. It also shows that a Dungey-like mechanism can happen on both large and small scales, giving more insight into where in the solar system this process could be taking place.“This is a remarkable result that changes how we think of Martian auroras and is another important step toward understanding why Mars and Earth have evolved so differently despite being governed by the same underlying physics.” said Shannon Curry, MAVEN’s principal investigator and a research scientist at the Laboratory for Atmospheric and Space Physics at the University of Colorado Boulder. “I am incredibly proud of our team's work on this discovery and excited to uncover new insights into the Red Planet and its evolution.”By finding out more about this process, scientists also are gaining a better understanding of how the solar environment interacts with the Red Planet as a whole, which is essential for future robotic and crewed missions.“I remember in graduate school discussing with my advisor how the cycling of crustal magnetic fields could work at Mars,” said Xu. “It’s incredible to be part of the team that found the answer to that question.”The MAVEN mission is part of NASA’s Mars Exploration Program portfolio. The mission’s principal investigator is based at the Laboratory for Atmospheric and Space Physics at the University of Colorado Boulder, which also is responsible for managing science operations and public outreach and communications. NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, manages the MAVEN mission. Lockheed Martin Space built the spacecraft and is responsible for mission operations. NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California provides navigation and Deep Space Network support.For more information on NASA’s MAVEN mission, visit:https://science.nasa.gov/mission/maven/Karen Fox / Alana JohnsonHeadquarters, Washington240-

item.title: 美国宇航局宇航员克里斯·威廉姆斯结束了空间站任务
item.description:
International Space Station Research Communications Team
After eight months aboard the International Space Station for his first mission, NASA astronaut Chris Williams is preparing to return to Earth. During his assignment, Williams contributed to research for new cancer treatments, advanced the production of materials to improve computers and electronics, ventured into the vacuum of space to complete two spacewalks, and much more. Williams’ work aboard the space station helped to improve life on Earth and prepare for future missions to the Moon and Mars.
Here are some of the research highlights from his mission:
NASA astronaut Chris Williams and ESA (European Space Agency) astronaut Sophie Adenot work to process DNA-inspired materials that could advance new cancer treatments for people on Earth. In space, these rod-shaped materials form more evenly and consistently, which may improve their performance and readiness for treatments on Earth. While there have been major advancements in cancer therapies, many treatments can affect the whole body and cause side effects without fully treating solid tumors. This research aims to enable targeted cancer therapies that reach deep into tumors, stay in the body longer, and release medicine in a more controlled way.
NASA astronaut Chris Williams conducts research to grow semiconductor crystals in space. In microgravity, researchers can grow more crystals of the desired size than can be produced on Earth. Previous research shows that space-grown crystals can offer increased performance to help advance technologies like high-performance computers, artificial intelligence, and medical devices. This research lays the groundwork for commercial semiconductor manufacturing in space and advances the semiconductor industry.
Learn more about In-Space Production of Semimetal-Semiconductor Composite Bulk Crystals in Microgravity (SUBSA-InSPA-SSCug).
NASA astronaut Chris Williams looks out of a cupola window at a red aurora glowing above the Earth. Since the 1960s, astronauts have photographed Earth from space to help scientists monitor the planet’s changing landscapes, natural disasters, and other features over time. Along the way, astronauts also have captured images of celestial objects such as comets, auroras, and the Milky Way.
NASA astronaut Chris Williams works with a special freezer aboard the International Space Station that keeps research samples at ultra-cold temperatures until they can return to Earth. Throughout each mission, astronauts collect biological samples like blood and urine to help scientists understand how long-duration spaceflight affects the human body. Observing crew members during their space missions and studying these frozen samples back on Earth helps NASA protect astronaut health during future missions to the Moon, Mars, and beyond.
Learn more about the Minus Eighty-Degree Laboratory Freezer for the International Space Station (MELFI) andHuman Research.
NASA astronauts Jack Hathaway and Chris Williams watch from the cupola windows as Northrop Grumman’s Cygnus XL cargo spacecraft approaches the International Space Station. The two played key roles in the capture of the spacecraft, which delivered approximately 11,000 pounds of supplies, including fresh food, life support equipment, and scientific research as part of NASA’sNorthrop Grumman Commercial Resupply Services 24 mission. Cargo missions help keep the space station operating and provide astronauts with the supplies they need to live, work, and conduct research in orbit.
NASA astronaut Chris Williams works on an investigation that tests the use of ultraviolet light to help prevent the formation of microbial colonies, called biofilms. Biofilms can clog and contaminate water systems, damage equipment, and pose health risks to astronauts. This research aims to keep surfaces cleaner and safeguard systems during long-duration space missions. Using UV light for sanitation also could reduce the need for chemical disinfectants in space, decreasing the risk of chemical exposure and eliminating difficulties in transporting or storing supplies.
Learn more about Germicidal Ultraviolet Light Biofilm Inhibition (GULBI).
NASA astronaut Chris Williams ventured outside the International Space Station for two spacewalks during his mission. In June, he helped make repairs to Canadarm2, a robotic arm that captures cargo spacecraft and deploys external research. In March, Williams prepared the orbiting laboratory for new solar arrays to be added to the station in a future spacewalk. Once installed, the final set of International Space Station Roll Out Solar Arrays (IROSA) will complete the full suite of additional solar power, increasing the station’s power generation by about 30% and enhancing support for scientific research and daily operations. The same solar array technology also powered NASA’sDouble Asteroid Redirection Testand could support future missions to the Moon and Mars.
Learn more about the

item.title: 奥林匹克山荣耀
item.description:
Snow-capped mountains carved by deep river valleys preside over northwestern Washington state.
Alpine glaciers, wild coastlines, temperate rainforests, and deep river valleys coexist on the Olympic Peninsula in the northwest corner of Washington state. Surrounded by blue waters, peaceful islands, and bustling population centers, its rugged interior remains a relatively remote bastion of wilderness.
The Olympic Mountains’ imposing terrain comes into focus in this oblique view of the region, captured by an astronaut aboard the International Space Station. The image is acomposite, made of several sequential, overlapping photos fused together into a panorama.Olympic National Parkencompasses the peninsula’s mountainous core, along with some stretches of the Pacific coastline. Much of theremaining areais either national forest, state-owned land, or tribal territory.
The rock making up the mountains mostly originated beneath the surface of the ocean. From about55 to 15 million years ago, layers of basalt from undersea eruptions and sand and mud transported seaward by rivers accumulated on the ocean bottom. This material wasscraped offthe Juan de Fuca plate as it subducted beneath the North American plate, with rock layers crumpling and rising up to 8,000 feet (2,440 meters) above sea level.
Tectonic forces continue to push the mountains skyward, but the countervailing force of erosion in this rainy, snowy corner of the country effectively cancels out the uplift. Snow at higher elevations feeds glaciers that carve out underlying rock.Glaciers in the Olympicsare retreating and thinning, however, and their numbers are declining. One study tallied255 glaciers and perennial snowfieldsin the range in 2015 and found that 35 glaciers and 16 perennial snowfields had disappeared in the preceding 35 years.
Other erosion is evidenced by the deep valleys radiating out from the snowy peaks. The Hoh, Queets, and Quinault rivers, draining west into the Pacific Ocean (bottom of the frame), are prominent in this view. These verdant valleys are known for theirtemperate rainforests, and the ancient forest in the Hoh River valley was once considered among the mostnaturally quiet placesin the U.S., uninterrupted by human-caused noise.
Flowing to the north, the Elwha River has a rich natural and human history, including some of the earliest Euro-Americanexplorationof the Olympics. Sponsored by a Seattle newspaper, an expedition from December 1889 to May 1890 crossed the mountain range from north to south, traveling up the Elwha valley and down the Quinault. The party spent several months in the Elwha Valley, their progress hindered by an unusually harsh and snowy winter.
In theearly 1900s, entrepreneurs saw economic opportunity in the valley. Two dams constructed on the river produced power for local industry. But the structures came with costs, such as blocking the migration of once-abundant trout and salmon to their spawning grounds. In 2011 and 2014, thedams were removedin what was then the largest such project in the U.S., and the process of restoring fish populations, seeding native plant communities, and replenishing sediment along the riverbankscommenced.
The mouth of the Elwha forms a delta in the Strait of Juan de Fuca, the waterway bordering the peninsula to the north. The U.S.-Canada border runs through the middle of this 11- to 17-mile-wide (18- to 27-kilometer-wide) channel, with Vancouver Island in British Columbia lying to the north. The straitconnectsthe Pacific Ocean with the Strait of Georgia and Puget Sound. Ship traffic uses the strait to access important West Coast ports, including Seattle and Tacoma, visible along the top-right edge of the image.
Astronaut photographsISS047-E-104138throughISS047-E-104144were acquired on May 6, 2016, with a Nikon D4 digital camera using a focal length of 400 millimeters. They are provided by the ISS Crew Earth Observations Facility and the Earth Science and Remote Sensing Unit at NASA Johnson Space Center. The images were taken by a member of theExpedition 47 crew. The images have been cropped and enhanced to improve contrast, and lens artifacts have been removed. TheInternational Space Station Programsupports the laboratory as part of theISS National Labto help astronauts take pictures of Earth that will be of the greatest value to scientists and the public, and to make those images freely available on the Internet. Additional images taken by astronauts and cosmonauts can be viewed at the NASA/JSCGateway to Astronaut Photography of Earth. Story by Lindsey Doermann.
Stay up-to-date with the latest content from NASA as we explore the universe and discover more about our home planet.
Along the northeast side of the Capital Beltway in Maryland, green spaces weave through the developed landscape.
The colonial communities of “America’s historic triangle” played defining roles in the road to American independence.
Phytoplankton added a milky blue hue to the waters of the Blac

item.title: 美国宇航局将在弗吉尼亚州展示该机构最新的风洞
item.description:
7 月 31 日星期五，媒体受邀前往位于弗吉尼亚州汉普顿的 NASA 兰利研究中心，参加媒体参观和飞行动力学研究设施的剪彩仪式，这是该机构 40 多年来的第一个新风洞。
该活动将包括简短的媒体报道：
该活动仅限个人参加，并向美国公民或合法永久居民的媒体成员开放。有关时间安排的信息将在活动临近时分享。 NASA 的媒体认证政策可在线获取。
要求亲自参加的媒体必须在下午 5 点之前回复。美国东部时间 7 月 29 日星期三。媒体回复必须发送至 Kimiko Booker (kimiko.s.booker@nasa.gov) 和 Brittny McGraw (brittny.v.mcgraw@nasa.gov)，并附上以下信息：
风洞的开放标志着美国宇航局和美国航空航天研究能力发展的一个重要里程碑。该最先进的设施将支持研究和技术开发，从而推进美国宇航局的航空、探索和科学目标，包括通过阿尔忒弥斯计划和月球基地的开发建立人类在月球表面的持续存在。
了解有关飞行动力学研究机构的更多信息：
卡米尔·加洛 / 罗布·玛格塔总部，华盛顿202-358-1600camille.m.gallo@nasa.gov/robert.j.margetta@nasa.gov
Kimiko Booker / Brittny McGrawNASA 兰利，汉普顿，弗吉尼亚州 757-506-5939 / 757-769-3763kimiko.s.booker@nasa.gov/brittny.v.mcgraw@nasa.gov

item.title: 在 NASA 支持下启动机器人服务任务
item.description:
继 7 月 21 日搭乘 SpaceX 猎鹰 9 号火箭从卡纳维拉尔角升空后，承载 NASA 支持的地球同步卫星机器人服务 (RSGS) 有效载荷的任务机器人飞行器 (MRV) 目前正在前往地球同步地球轨道的途中，在那里它将使用其先进的机器人技术为航天器提供服务。
RSGS 利用 NASA 的太空机器人专业知识，与该机构的更广泛目标保持一致，以提高美国在太空服务、组装和制造方面的能力，这些能力可应用于太空商业和探索。
RSGS 项目由美国国防高级研究计划局 (DARPA) 资助，采用由美国海军研究实验室设计和开发的双灵巧机械臂。 DARPA 提供了机械臂组件，用于集成到诺斯罗普·格鲁曼公司的 MRV 上，这是美国第一个多任务机器人太空服务商。该航天器将通过安装小型推进模块（称为任务扩展吊舱）来检查和升级在轨卫星，从而将现有航天器的使用寿命延长数年。
RSGS 将政府机构和行业聚集在一起，在太空中测试先进的机器人系统。根据与 DARPA 达成的跨机构协议，位于马里兰州格林贝尔特的 NASA 戈达德太空飞行中心于 2024 年开始支持 RSGS 任务。
美国宇航局对该任务的贡献利用了其维修任务的遗产，包括哈勃太空望远镜维修任务和国际空间站的机器人加油任务。 NASA 对 RSGS 计划的支持包括开发动态模拟和分析工具、用于性能验证的软件分析以及支持在轨高技术程序的飞行机器人操作员团队。数百颗卫星位于地球同步轨道上。其中，功能齐全的卫星通常会因为燃料耗尽或设备过时而提前退役。 RSGS 建立了美国延长在轨航天器寿命的关键能力，从而实现更具创新性和成本效益的任务设计。
作者：Colleen Wouters 美国宇航局戈达德太空飞行中心，马里兰州格林贝尔特

item.title: 美国国家航空航天局 (NASA) 为宇航员克里斯·威廉姆斯 (Chris Williams) 和船员归来设置了覆盖范围
item.description:
编者注：本通报于 2026 年 7 月 23 日更新，以反映任务时间表的变化。
NASA 宇航员克里斯·威廉姆斯 (Chris Williams) 和俄罗斯航天局 (Roscosmos) 宇航员谢尔盖·库德-斯维尔奇科夫 (Sergey Kud-Sverchkov) 和谢尔盖·米卡耶夫 (Sergei Mikaev) 即将结束在国际空间站上为期 241 天的任务。
宇航员及其联盟号 MS-28 航天器将于美国东部时间 7 月 26 日星期日凌晨 3 点 03 分从轨道实验室的 Rassvet 模块脱离，并于早上 6 点 25 分（当地时间下午 3 点 25 分）在哈萨克斯坦杰兹卡兹甘东南部的草原上进行降落伞辅助着陆。
美国宇航局的现场返回报道将通过各种平台进行直播。了解在哪里在线观看：
威廉姆斯和他的船员将完成 3,856 圈轨道飞行，并在返回地球之前行驶超过 1.02 亿英里。这次飞行标志着威廉姆斯和米卡耶夫的第一次任务，也是库德-斯维尔奇科夫的第二次任务。
着陆后，机组人员将乘坐直升机飞往救援队所在的哈萨克斯坦卡拉干达。威廉姆斯随后将返回美国宇航局位于休斯敦的约翰逊航天中心，而库德-斯维尔奇科夫和米卡耶夫将返回他们位于俄罗斯星城的训练基地。
NASA 的实时回传报道如下（所有时间均为东部时间，并可能根据实时操作而发生变化）：
上午 9:40 – 空间站远征队 74/75 指挥变更仪式开始报道。
库德-斯维尔奇科夫将把轨道综合体的指挥权移交给美国宇航局宇航员杰西卡·梅尔。当联盟号 MS-28 脱离对接时，第 75 次远征正式开始。
晚上 11:10 – 开始报道船员告别和舱口关闭。
凌晨 2:30 – 脱离对接报道开始。
凌晨 5:15 – 脱轨和着陆报道开始。
25 年多来，人们在国际空间站上持续生活和工作，推进科学知识进步，实现地球上不可能的研究突破。该空间站帮助美国宇航局了解和克服载人航天的挑战，扩大近地轨道的商业机会，并为长期月球任务（作为阿耳忒弥斯计划的一部分）和火星任务奠定基础。
要了解有关国际空间站研究、运营及其机组人员的更多信息，请访问：
约书亚·芬奇 / 吉米·拉塞尔总部，华盛顿202-358-1100joshua.a.finch@nasa.gov/james.j.russell@nasa.gov
Leah Cheshier / Anna Schneider 休斯顿约翰逊航天中心281-483-5111leah.d.cheshier@nasa.gov/anna.c.schneider@nasa.gov
